scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, researched directly against the connector's published OpenAPI/swagger spec (docs.bigmailer.io/llms.txt index + embedded swagger.json). Every read-oriented resource collection is now a stream: the original 5 (brands, users, contacts, lists, fields) plus 9 new brand-scoped/account-scoped resources (connections, message_types, segments, senders, templates, suppression_lists, bulk_campaigns, rss_campaigns, transactional_campaigns, test_campaigns). Every JSON-bodied CRUD mutation on a covered resource is now a writes.json action (create/update/delete for brands, contacts [+upsert], lists, fields, message_types, segments, senders, templates, users) -- capabilities.write flips to true. Campaign SEND/lifecycle actions (send a transactional email, send a test campaign, pause/unpause an RSS campaign) are excluded as destructive_admin (each dispatches real outbound email to real recipients; not a safe reverse-ETL data mutation). Contact-batch upload, suppression-list upload, and sender/bounce-domain DNS verification are excluded as requires_elevated_scope (binary/CSV file upload or external DNS-record verification side effects the declarative dialect has no multipart/verification-polling mechanism for). RSS campaign 'updates' sub-resources (auto-generated per-issue records, not directly authored) are excluded as non_data_endpoint.

excluded.reason: single-brand lookup by id; fully covered by the brands list stream
brand properties are an account-configuration namespace (arbitrary key/value settings), not a contact/CRM data resource meaningfully synced alongside brands/contacts; not part of this pass's scope
see GET /properties
see GET /properties
see GET /properties
see GET /properties
single-contact lookup by id/email; fully covered by the contacts list stream

uploads a batch file of up to 1,000 contacts processed asynchronously; the declarative write dialect has no multipart/file-upload body construction and no async job-polling mechanism (a single writes.json action is a synchronous per-record HTTP request)
polls the status of an async contact-batch upload job; meaningless without the batch-upload write action itself, which is excluded above
single-list lookup by id; fully covered by the lists list stream
single-field lookup by id; fully covered by the fields list stream
single-message-type lookup by id; fully covered by the message_types list stream
single-segment lookup by id; fully covered by the segments list stream
single-sender lookup by id; fully covered by the senders list stream
adds a bounce (MAIL FROM) DNS domain to a sender; a DNS-infrastructure side effect, not a BigMailer data resource mutation the declarative write dialect models
triggers external DNS record verification; a polling/side-effect action with no data record to write, not a fit for the create/update/delete write shape
triggers external DNS record verification for the sender identity itself; same reasoning as bounce-domains/verify
single-template lookup by id; fully covered by the templates list stream
uploads a suppression list as a file; the declarative write dialect has no multipart/file-upload body construction
single-suppression-list lookup by id; fully covered by the suppression_lists list stream
creates a bulk email campaign that (once scheduled/sent) dispatches real outbound email to real contacts; read-only sync of campaign metadata is in scope, authoring/scheduling sends is not
single-bulk-campaign lookup by id; fully covered by the bulk_campaigns list stream
updates a bulk campaign that may already be scheduled/sending; see POST /bulk-campaigns
creates a recurring RSS-triggered email campaign that dispatches real outbound email on every feed update; see POST /bulk-campaigns
single-RSS-campaign lookup by id; fully covered by the rss_campaigns list stream
updates an active/recurring RSS campaign; see POST /rss-campaigns
pauses a live recurring send schedule; a lifecycle-control action, not a data-record mutation
resumes a live recurring send schedule (immediately re-enables real outbound email dispatch); see pause
auto-generated per-feed-update campaign instances derived from the parent RSS campaign, not an independently authored data resource
single auto-generated RSS-update-campaign lookup; see the list form
creates a transactional campaign template that a later send action dispatches to real recipients; see POST /bulk-campaigns
single-transactional-campaign lookup by id; fully covered by the transactional_campaigns list stream
updates a live transactional campaign; see POST /transactional-campaigns
sends a real transactional email to a real recipient immediately; the archetypal unsafe reverse-ETL action for an email platform
sends a real test email to real recipient addresses immediately (this endpoint both creates AND sends in one call); not a safe data mutation
single-test-campaign lookup by id; fully covered by the test_campaigns list stream
single-connection lookup by id; fully covered by the connections list stream
single-user lookup by id; fully covered by the users list stream
